title: pittosporum rigidum
intro: Pittosporum rigidum, also known as the Australian cherry or New Zealand tobacco tree, is a flowering plant native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and skin conditions, thanks to its health benefits such as expectorant and antiseptic properties. Pittosporum rigidum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a versatile addition to natural remedies.
